task_completion.description: A rectangular selection is made on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashed line (marching ants) forming a rectangular shape is visible on the image canvas.

task_completion.description: Feathering is enabled for the selection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the tool options panel, the 'Feather edges' checkbox is ticked (ideally with a radius around 20), OR the corners of the rectangular selection appear rounded rather than sharp 90-degree angles.

task_completion.description: The selected region is filled with white.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large, solid white area is visible inside the boundaries of the selection on the image canvas.

task_completion.description: The edges of the white fill exhibit soft edge blending.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The transition from the white fill to the underlying original image is gradual and blurry (a soft edge), rather than a harsh, pixel-perfect sharp line.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the image editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image editor (e.g., GIMP) interface is visible with tools being selected and used, rather than terminal commands executing image manipulation scripts.

integrity.description: The modification was applied to the actual provided image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The white fill and soft edges are applied directly onto the landscape image within the editor's canvas, not on a blank canvas or a different image.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply open a pre-modified image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the step-by-step process of selecting, configuring feathering, and filling, rather than just loading a completed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires a feather radius of 20 pixels, which should produce a visible transition width of roughly 30-50 pixels. The fill must be bright white (brightness > 180). The soft edge must have a minimum transition width of 15px (no sharp edges). At least 5% of the image pixels must be modified. The environment is an image editor (GIMP) running at 1920x1080.
